root_text.sn29.6:1.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn29.6:1.3: “ko nu kho, bhante, hetu, ko paccayo, yena m’idhekacce opapātikā nāgā uposathaṁ upavasanti vossaṭṭhakāyā ca bhavantī”ti?
root_text.sn29.6:2.1: “Idha, bhikkhu, ekaccānaṁ opapātikānaṁ nāgānaṁ evaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn29.6:2.2: ‘mayaṁ kho pubbe kāyena dvayakārino ahumha, vācāya dvayakārino, manasā dvayakārino.
root_text.sn29.6:2.3: Te mayaṁ kāyena dvayakārino, vācāya dvayakārino, manasā dvayakārino, kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā opapātikānaṁ nāgānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapannā.
root_text.sn29.6:2.4: Sacajja mayaṁ kāyena sucaritaṁ careyyāma, vācāya … manasā sucaritaṁ careyyāma, evaṁ mayaṁ kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjeyyāma.
root_text.sn29.6:2.5: Handa mayaṁ etarahi kāyena sucaritaṁ carāma, vācāya … manasā sucaritaṁ carāmā’ti.
root_text.sn29.6:2.6: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhu, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo, yena m’idhekacce opapātikā nāgā uposathaṁ upavasanti vossaṭṭhakāyā ca bhavantī”ti.

translation_text.sn29.6:1.2: Seated to one side, that mendicant said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn29.6:1.3: “Sir, what is the cause, what is the reason why some spontaneously-born dragons keep the sabbath, having transformed their bodies?”
translation_text.sn29.6:2.1: “Mendicant, it’s when some spontaneously-born dragons think:
translation_text.sn29.6:2.2: ‘In the past we did both kinds of deeds by body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.sn29.6:2.3: When the body broke up, after death, we were reborn in the company of the spontaneously-born dragons.
translation_text.sn29.6:2.4: If today we do good things by body, speech, and mind, when the body breaks up, after death, we may be reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.sn29.6:2.5: Come, let us do good things by way of body, speech, and mind.’
translation_text.sn29.6:2.6: This is the cause, this is the reason why some spontaneously-born dragons keep the sabbath, having transformed their bodies.”
